title: Ignore Rules, Attributes, Line Endings, and Repository Content · Graph Lens: Line Ending Normalization
summary: Keep generated output, local configuration, binaries, and secrets out of history while preserving cross-platform text behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git line ending normalization increment in a content-boundary and normalization policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Control repository and working-tree line endings while separating attributes from core.autocrlf and reviewing renormalization.
Use attributes for text normalization, diff, merge, export, and binary behavior with check-attr evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

Line-ending settings change only how diffs are displayed. This does not resolve predict line ending normalization.

For predict line ending normalization, apply Line Ending Normalization without its stated preconditions, omit the a content-boundary and normalization policy trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Line Ending Normalization for a content-boundary and normalization policy.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Line Ending Normalization while producing a content-boundary and normalization policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: git-line-ending-normalization — Control repository and working-tree line endings while separating attributes from core.autocrlf and reviewing renormalization.
# Evidence: changed Git case 3
# hypothesis: the mismatch is isolated to one repository layer
git status --short --branch
git diff
git diff --staged
git log --graph --decorate --oneline -n 6
# observation: name worktree, index, ref, and graph evidence separately
